hero.headline:
Web, mobile and
.NET API products.
hero.subtitle: We design and ship production web, mobile, and .NET API products for teams that have outgrown templates and outsourced delivery. One studio, one standard, every line.
hero.trustStrip: .NET 8 · NEXT.JS · SWIFT · KOTLIN · POSTGRES · AZURE · AWS
hero.qualityMarker: Every line of code we ship is reviewed by someone whose name is on the contract.

services.title: Four ways we work with you.
services.subtitle: We keep the studio focused. Every engagement is led by a principal who writes code; no account managers, no junior bench, no resourcing surprises.

1.title: Web product engineering
1.description: Production Next.js apps with type-safe data layers and the details that matter — auth, billing, observability, accessibility. No prototypes left in production.

2.title: Mobile apps, native or cross-platform
2.description: Swift, Kotlin, or React Native — whichever matches the team that has to maintain it. Shipped to App Store and Play Store, not handed off as a zip file.

3.title: .NET API platforms
3.description: C# 12 / .NET 8 services with CQRS, idempotent jobs, and migrations you can read on a Friday. Built for teams who have to live with the code for five years.

4.title: Audits & rescues
4.description: A senior pair of eyes on an inherited codebase. Two-week structured review, written report, optional remediation. Often the cheapest engagement we run.

1.title: Discover.
1.description: Two weeks of structured conversations and prior-art review. We leave with a written problem statement and a list of the things you do not have to build.

2.title: Shape.
2.description: A working prototype of the riskiest screens, architecture decisions documented as ADRs, and a delivery plan with named owners.

3.title: Build.
3.description: Weekly visible progress in your repo. Production deploys from week three. Every Friday a written changelog with screenshots, decisions, and trade-offs.

4.title: Hand off.
4.description: The codebase your team inherits is the one they would have written if they had the time. Two weeks of paired work after launch, runbook included.

work.title:
Three engagements,
described plainly.
work.subtitle: Every active client is under NDA. The three below are the most recent we can describe in this much detail — full case studies on request.

1.title: A subscription billing platform for a European insurance broker.
1.body: .NET 8 services replacing a 12-year-old VB.NET monolith. Migrated 180k policies with zero customer-facing downtime.
1.outcome: Underwriting cycle time fell from nine days to under two. Two engineers run the entire platform.

2.title: A field-service mobile app for a national HVAC operator.
2.body: Offline-first Kotlin app for 600 technicians. Syncs jobs, parts, and photos over patchy LTE; built around the workflow, not the data model.
2.outcome: Paperwork time per job halved. Parts-misorder rate down from 8% to under 1%.

3.title: A real-time analytics dashboard for a logistics startup.
3.body: Server-rendered Next.js with a streaming GraphQL layer; loads under 400ms on 4G. Replaced a Tableau workbook the ops team had outgrown.
3.outcome: Dispatchers moved from end-of-shift reports to live decisions. Three roles consolidated.

tech.title: A focused toolbox.
tech.subtitle: We pick tools that will still be sensible in five years. The list below is intentionally short — we use it deeply, not broadly.
tech.scopeNote: We have opinions about microservices (mostly: don't, yet), about ORMs (yes, with care), and about feature flags (always). Ask us, on a call.

team.title:
A studio of two,
plus a long bench of friends.
team.stats: FOUNDED 2019 · BASED IN BUCHAREST, ROMANIA · SHIPPED 31 PRODUCTS · ONE OFFICE CAT

body.1: XeaCode is Filip Gagiu and one rotating principal, matched to the scope of the work. We have been writing software professionally since 2011 and running this studio since 2019.
body.2: Most of our work arrives by referral from teams we have shipped for. We keep the studio small intentionally — we would rather say no twice a quarter than reduce the level of attention we can give each project.
body.3: When the scope needs more capacity, we bring in a short list of people we have worked alongside for years. They sign the same contracts, write the same changelogs, and leave at the end of the engagement.

1.label: SENIORS ONLY
1.body: No bench, no juniors learning on your time. The person who shapes the engagement writes the code.

2.label: DOCUMENTED DECISIONS
2.body: Decisions live in ADRs and weekly changelogs, not Slack. Your team can read why we chose what we chose, two years from now.

3.label: ONE PROJECT AT A TIME
3.body: One project at a time, two at most. If we cannot give you a principal half the week, we say no.

4.label: EXIT AT WEEK TWO
4.body: Every engagement has a no-fee exit clause through week two. If the fit is wrong, both sides walk.

faq.title: The five we get asked most.

1.a: Most projects land between €60k and €240k, billed in fortnightly blocks. Audits are a fixed €18k. We send a written estimate before any contract; if the number changes during the project, we tell you in writing the same week.

2.a: Usually three to six weeks. We work one project at a time and never overbook. If the timing is wrong, we will say so on the first call and recommend someone else.

3.a: Yes, fully. The repo is in your GitHub org from day one. We commit under your CI, your branch protection, your review rules.

4.a: Yes, before the first technical conversation. We will also sign your MSA, your DPA, your SOC2-flavoured vendor questionnaire. We have lived through them all.

5.q: What if it is not working out?
5.a: Every engagement has a no-fee exit through week two and a 30-day termination clause after that. We would rather you leave cleanly than stay unhappy.

contact.title: Tell us about the work.
contact.subtitle: Two paragraphs is plenty for a first reply. We answer every message ourselves, usually inside one business day.
contact.preformNote: No automated replies. Filip reads every message.

form.name: YOUR NAME
form.successMessage: You will hear from Filip within one business day. Real address, real signature.
form.errorMessage: The form didn't send. Try again, or write to us directly at hello@xeacode.com.

footer.tagline: A senior independent software studio. Web, mobile, and .NET API products, shipped by the engineers who design them.
